Influence of self-absorption on isomeric ratio (IR)
Scattering of neutrons in oxygen existing in the sample
and subsequent capture in Eu can increase IR by < 1.%.
- If Cf-252 spectrum is described by Maxwellian
and the energy dependence of Eu-151 capture c-s is
then the effective energy of interaction of Eu nuclei
with the Cf SF spectrum neutrons is about 1.4 MeV.
On Fig.1 this data are plotted at energy EN=1.4 MeV

The Cf broad energy spectrum does not permit
unambiguous treatment of our results, but the increase
of relative yield of the isomer may be due to two
processes in the highly excited nucleus:
1) inclusion of the channel of inelastic scattering
with excitation of the Eu-151 level (196 keV) with
presumed spin 3/2+ ( this level was observed in
2) the possibility of existence of processes in which
enhancement of E2 transitions ar retardation of E1
transitions occurs, decreasing the dependence of
population of the ground and isomeric states on the
spin of the highly excited states.
